title: Norwegian Campaign (Operation Weserübung)
summary: Operation Weserübung was the German invasion of Denmark and Norway, launched simultaneously on 9 April 1940. Despite British and French intervention and fierce Norwegian resistance, Germany secured both countries within two months, gaining strategically vital North Sea bases and access to Swedish iron ore.
notable: The campaign saw the first major use of airborne troops (Fallschirmjäger) seizing Fornebu airport at Oslo. The Kriegsmarine suffered severe losses — 3 cruisers and 10 destroyers sunk — that crippled surface fleet operations for years. The British failure contributed to Neville Chamberlain's resignation and Churchill's appointment as Prime Minister.
outcome: German victory. Norway and Denmark occupied; Norway continued to resist until formal German surrender in May 1945.

casualties.allied: ~5,500 killed; Norwegian merchant navy suffered throughout occupation
